title: Corporate Insight Releases Report on Virtual Assistants in Retirement - Fidelity and Voya Earn Top Marks; Transparency, In-Chat Data and Live Escalation Emerge as Defining Features
author: Corporate Insight
description: Corporate Insight's Retirement Plan Monitor report evaluates virtual assistants across eight recordkeeper platforms, finding significant performance gaps. Fidelity and Voya Financial earn top marks for cross-platform strength, real-time account data delivery, and seamless live chat escalation. Most competitors fall short on in-chat data capabilities and consistent mobile availability, though adoption is gradually increasing as firms shift from human personas to AI-first branding.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Earned Excellent rating for Voya PAL with strongest in-chat account details including contributions, balances, activity and distribution data, plus direct live chat access.
Earned Excellent rating for Voya PAL with strongest in-chat account details including contributions, balances, activity and distribution data, plus direct live chat access.
Mentioned as one of few tools delivering in-chat account data, but no overall grade provided; rebranded Ask Lisa to Intelligent Virtual Assistant.

title: Deal Dispatch: QXO Nabs TopBuild For $17 Million, Voya Financial Urged To Evaluate Strategic Options, Pat McGrath Exits Chapter 11
author: Caroline Ryan
description: QXO agreed to acquire TopBuild for approximately $17 billion to expand its building products capabilities. Multiple companies announced strategic reviews including Voya Financial (urged by activist investor), Rayonier Advanced Materials, BNB Plus, Franklin Street Properties, and Investcorp Credit Management BDC. Notable completed transactions include ServiceNow's $7.75 billion acquisition of Armis, Eli Lilly's $7 billion deal for Kelonia Therapeutics, and Pat McGrath Labs exiting Chapter 11 bankruptcy under new ownership.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Announced major $17 billion acquisition of TopBuild to expand scale and capabilities, demonstrating growth strategy and market consolidation

title: Fidelity, Principal, T. Rowe Price, TIAA and Voya Lead Retirement Plan Sponsor Site Homepage Rankings, Corporate Insight Finds
author: Corporate Insight
description: Corporate Insight's latest research reveals that retirement plan recordkeepers are increasingly modernizing sponsor site homepages with task management tools, data visualization, and customization features. Fidelity, Principal, T. Rowe Price, TIAA, and Voya Financial received Excellent ratings, while Empower, Lincoln Financial, Merrill, Nationwide, Transamerica, and Vanguard earned Good ratings. Half of the 14 firms evaluated now offer homepage customization, with leading providers using interactive visualizations and action item surfacing to improve plan administration efficiency.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Received Excellent rating; recognized among top performers in modernizing sponsor site homepages.

title: Gator Capital Bets Big on VOYA With $9.3 Million Buy, According to Recent SEC Filing
author: Jake Lerch
description: Gator Capital Management initiated a new position in Voya Financial by purchasing 125,270 shares worth approximately $9.33 million in Q4 2025. The investment represents 1.89% of the fund's U.S. equity assets. Voya Financial, a diversified financial services provider, has shown strong recent performance with 35% stock appreciation over ten months and analyst forecasts predicting 11% year-over-year earnings growth in 2026, supported by a 2.5% dividend yield and $250 million share buyback authorization.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong recent performance with 35% appreciation over ten months, analyst forecasts showing 11% YoY earnings growth for 2026, solid 2.5% dividend yield, $250 million share buyback program, and institutional investor confidence demonstrated by Gator Capital's new $9.3 million position. However, some headwinds exist including medical cost inflation challenges and competitive pressures in asset management.

title: Voya Boosts Bet on Bonds With $9.3 Million BND Buy
author: Eric Trie
description: Voya Financial Advisors purchased 126,532 shares of Vanguard Total Bond Market ETF (BND), valued at $9.32 million, increasing its position to 13.89% of its reportable assets under management during the quarter.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Routine portfolio adjustment with strategic investment in a broad bond market ETF
